fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is close to actual fused size. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is close to actual fused size. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is close to actual fused size. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

findings.title: Over-matching risk in correspondences (one-to-many mappings)
findings.details: Worst one-to-many ratio is 53.70% in correspondences_dbpedia_metacritic.csv (max_right_per_left=17, rows_read=36142).

findings.title: Noisy attribute fusion or mapping for at least one attribute
findings.details: Lowest attribute accuracy is 'userScore' at 0.300 over count=10.

recommendations: Recalibrate fusion-size estimation: ensure singleton handling is consistent across estimate and actual, and verify that blocking/matching stages use the same inclusion criteria.
Reduce ambiguous matches: increase match threshold, add stricter blocking keys, and/or enforce one-to-one constraints via stable marriage / maximum-weight matching post-processing.
For low-accuracy attributes, verify schema mappings and normalization (e.g., numeric parsing, categorical harmonization); consider attribute-specific fusion rules and source trust weights.

summary.note: Human-review artifacts created from fused output; lineage mapped via _fusion_sources when available; diffs computed only if testset could be parsed and aligned.

Source code for PyDI.informationextraction.evaluation
"""Evaluation framework for information extraction in PyDI.
This module provides an evaluator to compare extracted (predicted)
attributes against an expected/validation dataset with common IR-style metrics.
It supports per-attribute evaluation functions (e.g., exact match,
tokenized string match, numeric tolerance) and produces both attribute-level
and dataset-level (micro/macro) metrics, including precision, recall, F1, and
non-null accuracy.
"""

__future__

annotations

,
